metadata.title:
GRADE
CROSSINGS


ABOLISHED

PLAN OF NORTH COAST HAD
ELIMINATED THESE MAN


KILLERS.

pages.raw_text:
In response to the inquiry as to
what the North Coast railway pro-
posed to grant in the way of grade
separation and common user con-
cessions, in return for the franchise
1t asks, an investigation made to-
day shows that the city council and
the North Coast had practically
agreed upon grade separation for
the nearly so crossings of that road
leading into the city. In caring for
this grade separation the company
expected to expend for that one
item $400, 900 to $500, 900.


The Milwaukee likewise expects
to have practically complete grade
separation. but its plans had not
been examined in detail when the
discussion Of the franchise ceased
by the city council.


As to the common user clause the
two roads had agreed to permit the
Joint occupation of the union ter
minal station and terminals be-
tween Center street on the east
and Monroe street on the west, al
though the proposed franchise Of
the joint terminal company ex-
tended only as far west as Lincoln
street.


The question of the extent of the
terminals open to the common user
provision had never been discussed.
as the NEGOTIATIONS had been ended
before that point was reached.
